request.question: Does CRISPR knockout of TP53 in iPSC-derived cardiomyocytes increase doxorubicin sensitivity measured by LDH release?

hypothesis.original_hypothesis: Does CRISPR knockout of TP53 in iPSC-derived cardiomyocytes increase doxorubicin sensitivity measured by LDH release?

literature_qc.explanation: The retrieved papers do not provide any relevant data on the CRISPR knockout of TP53 specifically in iPSC-derived cardiomyocytes or assess doxorubicin sensitivity through LDH release. They touch on related topics within cancer and cardiovascular diseases but do not overlap sufficiently with the specific experimental context outlined in the hypothesis.

plan.objective: Determine the sensitivity of CRISPR knockout TP53 iPSC-derived cardiomyocytes to doxorubicin as measured by LDH release.
plan.experimental_design: iPSC-derived cardiomyocytes will be subjected to CRISPR knockout of TP53. The sensitivity to doxorubicin will be assessed by measuring LDH release from treated and untreated cells. Comparisons will be made between CRISPR-mediated knockout and wild-type controls.
controls: Doxorubicin treatment in wild-type iPSC-derived cardiomyocytes with a known LDH release response curve.
Untreated iPSC-derived cardiomyocytes without CRISPR KO or doxorubicin exposure.

step_by_step_protocol.description:
DAY 0 (PREP) | 48 h | Cell Culture
Thaw iPSC-derived cardiomyocytes and expand them in 6-well plates containing 2 mL of cardiac differentiation medium (CDM) per well at 37°C, 5% CO2 for 48 h.
Expected outcome: Cells are at approximately 70-80% confluency after 48 h.

DAY 1 | 2 h | CRISPR Design
Design sgRNA targeting TP53 using CRISPR design software (e.g., Benchling).
Expected outcome: sgRNA sequences designed with efficiency predictions.

DAY 1 | 1 h | sgRNA Synthesis
Order synthesized sgRNA for TP53 from Integrated DNA Technologies, at a final concentration of 100 µM in provided buffer.
Expected outcome: sgRNA delivered in 1 mL aliquots.

DAY 1 | 1 h | Electroporation
Electroporate iPSC-derived cardiomyocytes with 0.5 µg of sgRNA and 0.5 µg Cas9 protein using the Neon Transfection System (Thermo Fisher) with a 1100 V, 20 ms pulse.
Expected outcome: >70% transfection efficiency as measured by a separate fluorescent marker in a control experiment.

DAY 1 | 48 h | Recovery
Culture electroporated cells in 2 mL CDM at 37°C, 5% CO2 for 48 h to promote recovery.
Expected outcome: Healthy, confluent cell appearance.

DAY 3 | 7 days | Selection
Add 1 µg/mL puromycin to the medium to select successfully transfected cardiomyocytes for 7 days.
Expected outcome: Cell population enriched for knockout cells by day 10.

DAY 10 | 2 h | Validation of Knockout
Extract genomic DNA from cells using DNeasy Blood & Tissue Kit (Qiagen) to validate TP53 knockout via PCR amplification and Sanger sequencing.
Expected outcome: Successful amplification of TP53 regions showing insertion/deletion mutations.

DAY 10 | 30 min | LDH Cytotoxicity Assay Preparation
Prepare LDH assay master mix according to kit instructions (e.g., Takara Bio, 50 µL master mix).
Expected outcome: Master mix prepared and ready for use.

DAY 10 | 24 h | Doxorubicin Treatment
Treat knockout and control cardiomyocytes with 0.5 µM doxorubicin for 24 h at 37°C in 5% CO2.
Expected outcome: Cell morphology monitored for signs of toxicity.

DAY 11 | 1 h | LDH Assay
Measure LDH release into culture supernatants post-doxorubicin treatment using a 96-well plate, adding 50 µL of assay reagent per 50 µL supernatant per well.
Expected outcome: LDH activity measured in absorbance at 490 nm.

DAY 11 | 1 h | Curve Fitting
Fit LDH release data to a standard curve to quantify doxorubicin sensitivity using GraphPad Prism 9.
Expected outcome: Concentrations corresponding to LDH release values determined.

DAY 11 | 30 min | Statistical Analysis
Perform unpaired t-tests to compare doxorubicin-induced LDH release in TP53 knockout versus control cardiomyocytes.
Expected outcome: Statistical significance determined with p-values reported.

DAY 11 | 2 h | Documentation
Compile experiment data and write detailed report summarizing findings, methodology, and interpretation.
Expected outcome: Comprehensive experimental report prepared.

DAY 12 | 1 h | Review Findings
Present findings to lab group for feedback and potential further exploration.
Expected outcome: Feedback gathered for future experiments and improvements.

DAY 12 | 1 h | Plan Follow-Up Experiments
Design follow-up experiments based on preliminary results and feedback received.
Expected outcome: Outline prepared for next phase experiments.

DAY 12 | 30 min | Data Storage
Store all raw data and experimental notes in a secure digital format for reproducibility.
Expected outcome: Data securely archived.

DAY 12 | 1 h | Calculate Sensitivity Threshold
Determine LDH release threshold for sensitivity classification based on established guidelines.
Expected outcome: Threshold established for future comparative studies.

assumptions: CRISPR knockout of TP53 will influence doxorubicin sensitivity in cardiomyocytes.
LDH release is a reliable measure of cellular damage from doxorubicin.
The iPSC-derived cardiomyocyte model accurately reflects in vivo conditions.
decision_criteria: Continue if LDH release from knockout is significantly higher compared to controls attenuation by doxorubicin >30% across all concentration levels.
Proceed with in vivo validation if there is a discernible difference in doxorubicin sensitivity between groups.
reproducibility_notes: Maintain consistent passage number of iPSC cultures.
Use identical batches of reagents and consumables for all assays.
Ensure calibration of pipettes and spectrophotometer is performed prior to experiments.

validation.primary_endpoint: Post-thaw viability percentage at immediate and 24h checkpoints.
validation.success_threshold: not specified improvement over comparator in replicate-confirmed data.
failure_conditions: No meaningful viability gain in replicate-confirmed runs.
Intervention increases delayed viability loss at 24h.
validation.suggested_statistical_comparison: Two-group comparison against comparator using t-test or Mann-Whitney based on normality.
validation.minimum_replicates_or_design_note: Minimum three biological replicates before execution-level claim.

cro_ready_brief.objective: Determine the sensitivity of CRISPR knockout TP53 iPSC-derived cardiomyocytes to doxorubicin as measured by LDH release.
scope_of_work: Execute pilot-first HeLa cryopreservation comparison between DMSO baseline and trehalose arm.
Deliver replicate-confirmed viability analysis and mitigation traceability report.
cro_ready_brief.sample_count: At least 3 biological replicates per arm
cro_ready_brief.materials_responsibility: CRO to quote and confirm all materials before ordering.
questions_for_cro: Can you support controlled-rate freezing logs in each run?
Can you include replicate-level batch metadata in the final report?
